videos.title: Mirror Images and Reflections | Geometry
videos.description: Understand how mirror images work and how to draw reflections of shapes.
learningObjectives: Understand what a mirror image is
See how left and right flip in a mirror
Draw the mirror image of a simple shape
keyConceptsCovered: Left and right are swapped in a mirror
The mirror line is the line of symmetry

videos.title: What is Symmetry? | Line of Symmetry for Kids
videos.description: Learn what symmetry means and how to find lines of symmetry in shapes and objects around you.
learningObjectives: Connect line of symmetry to mirror images
See that each half is a mirror image of the other
keyConceptsCovered: The line of symmetry acts like a mirror

videos.title: Matching and Same-Different | Comparing Shapes and Patterns
videos.description: Practise matching identical shapes and spotting what is the same and what is different. This skill helps you see symmetry!
learningObjectives: Match shapes and their mirror images
Spot differences between a shape and its reflection
Practise visual comparison for mirror images
keyConceptsCovered: Mirror images look the same but are flipped

videos.title: Equal Halves and Parts | Folding Shapes in Half
videos.description: Learn what it means to divide a shape into two equal halves. When both halves match perfectly, that is symmetry!
learningObjectives: See that folded halves are mirror images
keyConceptsCovered: Each half is a reflection of the other
The fold line is the mirror line

excellent_performance.description: For students who scored >= 90% on readiness
excellent_performance.rationale: Core mirror image concepts for strong students

ready_performance.description: For students who scored 80-89% on readiness
ready_performance.rationale: Complete mirror image coverage with matching practice

partial_readiness.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness
partial_readiness.rationale: Matching review first, then mirror image concepts with folding practice

after_prerequisite_review.description: For students who scored < 50% but completed prerequisites

questions.question: When you look at the letter 'b' in a mirror, it looks like which letter?
questions.explanation: In a mirror, left and right are flipped. So the letter 'b' looks like 'd' in a mirror!

usageNotes.teacherGuidance: Use a real mirror in class to demonstrate reflections. Have students hold up letters, numbers, and shapes to see their mirror images.
usageNotes.parentGuidance: Stand with your child in front of a mirror. Try waving different hands and writing letters to explore how reflections work.
usageNotes.studentGuidance: Try holding a book in front of a mirror. Notice how the words look backwards! That is what a mirror image is.
